title: EEG responses to continuous naturalistic speech
description: This dataset comprises EEG recordings from healthy adults listening to naturalistic continuous speech from an audiobook. Brain activity was recorded using a 128-channel ActiveTwo EEG system while subjects listened to segments from "The Old Man and the Sea," with concurrent linguistic annotations including word and phoneme timing. The dataset supports investigation of cortical responses to naturalistic speech and phoneme-level processing mechanisms.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired using a 128-channel ActiveTwo system (BioSemi) at 512 Hz sampling rate. Recordings were unfiltered and unreferenced. Linguistic stimuli consisted of audiobook segments with forced-alignment annotations (word and phoneme timing) generated using Prosodylab-Aligner software and verified by visual inspection.
